item.title: Seriously, Tucker Carlson? Come On
item.description: Media figures who have turned against Trump only in recent weeks have forfeited the right to be taken seriously in the future.
item.dc:creator: David A. Graham

item.title: Theft Is Now Progressive Chic
item.description: In some left-wing corners of the commentariat, moral rectitude is out. Flagrant disregard of the social contract is in.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Chatterton Williams

item.title: An Unsettling Anti-Slavery Memorial
item.description: One monument’s story shows just how quickly a nation’s ideals can erode.
item.dc:creator: Jake Lundberg

item.title: Israel Could Have What It Most Wants in Lebanon
item.description: It just has to give up territorial ambitions and work with the Lebanese government to disarm Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Hussein Ibish

item.title: The Kash Patel Fallout
item.description: An interview with theAtlanticstaff writer Sarah Fitzpatrick about her reporting
item.dc:creator: Hanna Rosin

item.title: The Flip-Phone Cleanse
item.description: I spent a month with a group of people who aspire to a state of offline bliss.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlyn Tiffany

item.title: Cubans’ Despair
item.description: Economic desperation has morphed into frustration, including at those purporting to help.
item.dc:creator: Gisela Salim-Peyer

item.title: The Posting Will Continue Until Morale Improves
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Chait

item.title: The New Plastic-Surgery Playbook
item.description: Celebrities aren’t just admitting they got work done—they’re showing all the details.
item.dc:creator: Rheana Murray

item.title: Today’sAtlanticTrivia: American Gerontocracy
item.description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
item.dc:creator: Drew Goins

item.title: Trump Voters Like Marco Rubio More and More
item.description: And J. D. Vance less and less
item.dc:creator: Sarah Longwell

item.title: Life Isn’t Easy in the OnlyFans Economy
item.description: TV is finally considering the relentless, creative work of making a living online.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Gilbert

item.title: The Questionable Triumph of the ‘Baling Wire Hippies’
item.description: Stewart Brand’sWhole Earth Catalogwas seen as a countercultural milestone, but his new book reveals his alliances with the powerful.
item.dc:creator: Alec Nevala-Lee

item.title: If I Tried to Escape, I Would Be Killed
item.dc:creator: Shelly Kittleson

item.title: The Big Question the Fed-Chair Hearing Leaves Open
item.description: At yesterday’s confirmation hearing, Kevin Warsh remained tactically opaque.
item.dc:creator: Will Gottsegen

item.title: MAHA Swing Voters Are an Illusion
item.description: Politicians are fooling themselves about the political power of health-conscious moms.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Florko

item.title: On the Brink of Global Recession
item.description: The economist Adam Posen on the effect of the war in Iran on the world’s economy and the darkening economic outlook for the United States. Plus: A shifting partisan balance of power andAsk Not: The Kennedys and the Women They Destroyed, by Maureen Callahan.
item.dc:creator: David Frum

item.title: The Virginia Gerrymander Disenfranchises Republicans
item.description: Republicans seem to have expected that Democrats would continue to follow rules they had long since enthusiastically abandoned.
item.dc:creator: Adam Serwer

item.title: Photos: Reclaimed by Nature
item.description: On Earth Day, I wanted to share images of the incredible resilience of nature: the many ways that plants, animals, and natural processes reclaim abandoned human places and find ways to thrive.
item.dc:creator: Alan Taylor

item.title: Hegseth to the Troops: We Are Bringing Back the Flu!
item.description: Grab your cough drops. We’re going to battle.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Petri
